children.heading: .—Organization
children.label: a .—Organization
children.text: The Federal Maritime Commission is an independent establishment of the United States Government.

children.heading: .—Composition
children.label: 1 .—Composition
children.text: The Commission is composed of 5 Commissioners, appointed by the President by and with the advice and consent of the Senate. Not more than 3 Commissioners may be appointed from the same political party.

children.heading: .—Terms
children.label: 2 .—Terms
children.text: The term of each Commissioner is 5 years. When the term of a Commissioner ends, the Commissioner may continue to serve until a successor is appointed and qualified, but for a period not to exceed 2 years. Except as provided in paragraph (3), no individual may serve more than 3 terms.

children.heading: .—Vacancies
children.label: 3 .—Vacancies
children.text: A vacancy shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment. An individual appointed to fill a vacancy is appointed only for the unexpired term to which such individual is appointed. An individual appointed to fill a vacancy may serve 3 terms in addition to the remainder of the term for which such individual was appointed.

children.heading: .—Limitation on relationships with regulated entities
children.label: A .—Limitation on relationships with regulated entities
children.text: A Commissioner may not have a pecuniary interest in, hold an official relation to, or own stocks or bonds of any entity the Commission regulates under chapter 401 of this title.

children.heading: .—Limitation on other activities
children.label: B .—Limitation on other activities
children.text: A Commissioner may not engage in another business, vocation, or employment.

children.heading: .—Removal
children.label: 5 .—Removal
children.text: The President may remove a Commissioner for inefficiency, neglect of duty, or malfeasance in office.

children.heading: .—Designation
children.label: 1 .—Designation
children.text: The President shall designate one of the Commissioners as Chairman.

children.heading: .—General authority
children.label: 2 .—General authority
children.text: The Chairman is the chief executive and administrative officer of the Commission. In carrying out the duties and powers of the Commission (other than under paragraph (3)), the Chairman is subject to the policies, regulatory decisions, findings, and determinations of the Commission.

children.label: i
children.text: appoint and supervise officers and employees of the Commission;

children.label: ii
children.text: appoint the heads of major organizational units (with such appointments subject to the approval of the Commission);

children.label: iii
children.text: distribute the business of the Commission among personnel and organizational units;

children.label: iv
children.text: supervise the expenditure of money for administrative purposes;

children.label: v
children.text: assign Commission personnel, including Commissioners, to perform duties and powers delegated by the Commission under ; andsection 46104 of this title

children.label: vi
children.text: prepare and submit to the President and the Congress requests for appropriations for the Commission (with such requests subject to the approval of the Commission).

children.heading: .—Nonapplication
children.label: B .—Nonapplication
children.text: Subparagraph (A) (other than clause (v)) does not apply to personnel employed regularly and full-time in the offices of Commissioners other than the Chairman.

children.heading: .—Delegation
children.label: 4 .—Delegation
children.text: The Chairman may designate officers and employees under the Chairman’s jurisdiction to perform duties and powers of the Chairman, subject to the Chairman’s supervision and direction.

children.heading: .—Seal
children.label: d .—Seal
children.text: The Commission shall have a seal which shall be judicially recognized.
